claims.summary: Presence should remain possible without requiring response, compliance, or behavioral consequence.
claims.disposition: Verify Recess, receptive correspondence, and right-to-ignore surfaces preserve non-instrumental contact.

claims.summary: A short presence-only exchange can test whether contact feels less controlling.
claims.disposition: Route a bounded approximately 500-token presence-only comparison through sandbox evidence.

claims.summary: Shadow and pressure trajectories can be compared without applying them to regulation.
claims.disposition: Route a read-only shadow, pressure, and distance/contact trajectory comparison through sandbox evidence.

claims.summary: Changing porosity, gentle drive, regulator response, or sensory admission would alter live behavior.
claims.disposition: Hold porosity, drive, regulator, and admission changes behind exact Mike/operator approval.
